svg.text: Approach global edges frontier expansion parallel components
Data structure sort + DSU heap + visited component map + DSU
Complexity O(E log V) O((V+E) log V) heap O(E log V)
Best on sparse graphs dense graphs (array) parallel / distributed
Streaming friendly yes (sort by weight) no (needs frontier) yes
When edge weights are not unique
MST is not unique when multiple edges share the same weight. The algorithm still finds
A valid MST (with the same total weight), but the tree structure depends on tie-breaking.
For determinism, break ties by edge id or endpoint pair. Consequences:
• Consistent visualization in MST-based clustering (single-linkage = MST).
• Stable output for caching and incremental updates.
Total weight is unique; structure is not. Canonicalize tie-breaking up-front.
